# ===================================================================
# COMPREHENSIVE EMPLOYMENT FORECASTING FOR COLLEGE & UNIVERSITY STUDENTS
# Multiple metrics, all ages, 10-year forecast, linear regression
# ===================================================================
```{r}

# === Setup ===
req_pkgs <- c("readr","dplyr","stringr","ggplot2","tidyr","scales","patchwork")
to_install <- setdiff(req_pkgs, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)
lapply(req_pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE)

# === Load data ===
data_path <- "Employed and Unemployed Dataset.csv"
raw <- readr::read_csv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# === Define target metrics ===
# Note: "Student unemployed or Other" combines unemployed with other categories
target_metrics <- c(
  "University student employed",
  "College student employed",
  "Student unemployed or Other"  # This includes both college & university unemployed
)

# === Filter and aggregate across ALL age groups ===
# Keep high-quality data and aggregate across all individual ages
filtered <- raw %>%
  filter(
    `Labour force status of students and non-students` %in% target_metrics,
    is.na(SYMBOL) | SYMBOL != "x",  # Exclude missing
    is.na(STATUS) | STATUS %in% c("A","B"),  # Keep best quality
    !is.na(VALUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = as.integer(stringr::str_sub(REF_DATE, 1, 4)),
    Metric = `Labour force status of students and non-students`
  ) %>%
  # Aggregate across all age groups for each metric and year
  group_by(Year, Metric) %>%
  summarise(
    Rate = mean(VALUE, na.rm = TRUE),  # Average rate across age groups
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Metric, Year)

# Check how many data points per metric
data_summary <- filtered %>%
  group_by(Metric) %>%
  summarise(n_periods = n(), .groups = "drop")

print("=== Data Summary ===")
print(data_summary)

# === Create separate datasets for each metric ===
univ_employed <- filtered %>% filter(Metric == "University student employed")
college_employed <- filtered %>% filter(Metric == "College student employed")
student_unemployed <- filtered %>% filter(Metric == "Student unemployed or Other")

# === Fit linear models for each ===
model_univ_emp <- lm(Rate ~ Year, data = univ_employed)
model_coll_emp <- lm(Rate ~ Year, data = college_employed)
model_stud_unemp <- lm(Rate ~ Year, data = student_unemployed)

# === Generate 10-year forecasts ===
horizon <- 10
last_year <- max(filtered$Year, na.rm = TRUE)
future_years <- data.frame(Year = seq.int(last_year + 1, last_year + horizon))

# Function to generate forecast with intervals
generate_forecast <- function(model, metric_name, historical_data, future_years) {
  # In-sample fitted
  hist_fit <- cbind(
    historical_data,
    predict(model, newdata = historical_data, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95)
  )
  names(hist_fit)[(ncol(hist_fit)-2):ncol(hist_fit)] <- c("fit","lwr","upr")
  hist_fit$Type <- "Historical"
  
  # Future forecast with prediction intervals
  fc_pred <- cbind(
    future_years,
    as.data.frame(predict(model, newdata = future_years, interval = "prediction", level = 0.95))
  )
  names(fc_pred)[2:4] <- c("fit","lwr","upr")
  fc_pred$Metric <- metric_name
  fc_pred$Type <- "Forecast"
  
  list(historical = hist_fit, forecast = fc_pred)
}

# Generate forecasts for all metrics
fc_univ_emp <- generate_forecast(model_univ_emp, "University student employed", univ_employed, future_years)
fc_coll_emp <- generate_forecast(model_coll_emp, "College student employed", college_employed, future_years)
fc_stud_unemp <- generate_forecast(model_stud_unemp, "Student unemployed or Other", student_unemployed, future_years)

# === Print model summaries ===
cat("\n=== MODEL SUMMARIES ===\n\n")

cat("1. UNIVERSITY STUDENTS EMPLOYED\n")
print(summary(model_univ_emp))
cat("\n")

cat("2. COLLEGE STUDENTS EMPLOYED\n")
print(summary(model_coll_emp))
cat("\n")

cat("3. STUDENTS UNEMPLOYED OR OTHER\n")
print(summary(model_stud_unemp))
cat("\n")

# === Print forecast tables ===
cat("\n=== 10-YEAR FORECASTS ===\n\n")

print_forecast_table <- function(fc_data, title) {
  cat(paste0("\n", title, "\n"))
  cat(paste0(rep("-", nchar(title)), collapse = ""), "\n")
  fc_table <- fc_data$forecast %>%
    transmute(
      Year,
      Forecast = round(fit, 2),
      Lower_95CI = round(lwr, 2),
      Upper_95CI = round(upr, 2)
    )
  print(fc_table, row.names = FALSE)
}

print_forecast_table(fc_univ_emp, "University Students Employed (%)")
print_forecast_table(fc_coll_emp, "College Students Employed (%)")
print_forecast_table(fc_stud_unemp, "Students Unemployed or Other (%)")

# ===================================================================
# VISUALIZATION: Create 3 graphs with logical groupings
# ===================================================================

# === GRAPH 1: University vs College (EMPLOYED comparison) ===
plot_data_employed <- bind_rows(
  fc_univ_emp$historical %>% mutate(Metric = "University"),
  fc_univ_emp$forecast %>% select(-Metric) %>% mutate(Metric = "University", Rate = NA),
  fc_coll_emp$historical %>% mutate(Metric = "College"),
  fc_coll_emp$forecast %>% select(-Metric) %>% mutate(Metric = "College", Rate = NA)
)

p1 <- ggplot() +
  # Historical points
  geom_point(
    data = plot_data_employed %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = Rate, color = Metric),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  # Historical fitted lines
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_employed %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Metric),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # Forecast lines
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_employed %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Metric),
    linewidth = 1,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  # Prediction intervals (shaded)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = plot_data_employed %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Metric),
    alpha = 0.15
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("University" = "#1f77b4", "College" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("University" = "#1f77b4", "College" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "%")) +
  labs(
    title = "Employment Rates: University vs. College Students",
    subtitle = "10-year forecast with 95% prediction intervals",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Employment Rate (%)",
    color = "Student Type",
    fill = "Student Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# === GRAPH 2: Employed vs Unemployed (UNIVERSITY students) ===
plot_data_univ <- bind_rows(
  fc_univ_emp$historical %>% mutate(Status = "Employed"),
  fc_univ_emp$forecast %>% select(-Metric) %>% mutate(Status = "Employed", Rate = NA),
  fc_stud_unemp$historical %>% mutate(Status = "Unemployed/Other"),
  fc_stud_unemp$forecast %>% select(-Metric) %>% mutate(Status = "Unemployed/Other", Rate = NA)
)

p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_data_univ %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = Rate, color = Status),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_univ %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Status),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_univ %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Status),
    linewidth = 1,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = plot_data_univ %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Status),
    alpha = 0.15
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Employed" = "#2ca02c", "Unemployed/Other" = "#d62728")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Employed" = "#2ca02c", "Unemployed/Other" = "#d62728")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "%")) +
  labs(
    title = "Student Employment Status Comparison",
    subtitle = "University students employed vs. all students unemployed/other (10-year forecast)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Rate (%)",
    color = "Status",
    fill = "Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# === GRAPH 3: ALL FOUR METRICS (Combined overview) ===
plot_data_all <- bind_rows(
  fc_univ_emp$historical %>% mutate(Metric_Label = "Univ Employed"),
  fc_univ_emp$forecast %>% mutate(Rate = NA, Metric_Label = "Univ Employed"),
  fc_coll_emp$historical %>% mutate(Metric_Label = "College Employed"),
  fc_coll_emp$forecast %>% mutate(Rate = NA, Metric_Label = "College Employed"),
  fc_stud_unemp$historical %>% mutate(Metric_Label = "Unemployed/Other"),
  fc_stud_unemp$forecast %>% mutate(Rate = NA, Metric_Label = "Unemployed/Other")
)

p3 <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_data_all %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = Rate, color = Metric_Label),
    size = 2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_all %>% filter(Type == "Historical"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Metric_Label),
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data_all %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, y = fit, color = Metric_Label),
    linewidth = 0.9,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = plot_data_all %>% filter(Type == "Forecast"),
    aes(x = Year, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Metric_Label),
    alpha = 0.12
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "%")) +
  labs(
    title = "All Student Employment Metrics: 10-Year Forecast",
    subtitle = "University employed, college employed, and unemployed/other students",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Rate (%)",
    color = "Metric",
    fill = "Metric"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# === Display all plots ===
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)

# Optional: Combine plots using patchwork
# combined_plot <- (p1 / p2) | p3
# print(combined_plot)

# === Save results to CSV ===
forecast_results <- bind_rows(
  fc_univ_emp$forecast %>% mutate(Metric = "University student employed"),
  fc_coll_emp$forecast %>% mutate(Metric = "College student employed"),
  fc_stud_unemp$forecast %>% mutate(Metric = "Student unemployed or Other")
) %>%
  select(Metric, Year, Forecast = fit, Lower_95CI = lwr, Upper_95CI = upr) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Forecast, Lower_95CI, Upper_95CI), ~ round(.x, 2)))

readr::write_csv(forecast_results, "student_employment_forecasts_10yr.csv")
cat("\n✓ Forecast results saved to: student_employment_forecasts_10yr.csv\n")

# === Summary statistics ===
cat("\n=== TREND ANALYSIS ===\n")
trend_summary <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("University Employed", "College Employed", "Unemployed/Other"),
  Slope_Per_Year = c(
    coef(model_univ_emp),
    coef(model_coll_emp),
    coef(model_stud_unemp)
  ),
  R_Squared = c(
    summary(model_univ_emp)$r.squared,
    summary(model_coll_emp)$r.squared,
    summary(model_stud_unemp)$r.squared
  ),
  P_Value = c(
    summary(model_univ_emp)$coefficients[2,4],
    summary(model_coll_emp)$coefficients[2,4],
    summary(model_stud_unemp)$coefficients[2,4]
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    Slope_Per_Year = round(Slope_Per_Year, 4),
    R_Squared = round(R_Squared, 3),
    P_Value = round(P_Value, 4),
    Trend_Direction = ifelse(Slope_Per_Year > 0, "↑ Increasing", "↓ Decreasing"),
    Significance = ifelse(P_Value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

print(trend_summary)

cat("\n=== COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Generated 3 plots:\n")
cat("  1. University vs College (Employed comparison)\n")
cat("  2. Employed vs Unemployed (All students)\n")
cat("  3. All metrics combined (Overview)\n")
cat("\nForecasts extend 10 years into the future with 95% prediction intervals.\n")

# === Save plots as PNG ===
ggplot2::ggsave("employment_university_vs_college.png", plot = p1,
                width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

ggplot2::ggsave("employment_status_comparison.png", plot = p2,
                width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

ggplot2::ggsave("employment_all_metrics_10yr.png", plot = p3,
                width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```
